title: Datadog の 503 エラー：原因と解決策
reasons: Most technical claims regarding Datadog 503 error causes and troubleshooting steps are well-supported by official documentation or reputable sources.
The article correctly identifies standard HTTP 503 error conditions and Datadog-specific Agent commands and configuration.
References to GitHub issues provide relevant real-world examples of problems encountered by users.
The importance of regional site settings and API keys is accurately conveyed and sourced.

sources.title: 503 Service Unavailable - HTTP - MDN Web Docs
sources.claim: When a 503 error occurs, it indicates that the server side is temporarily not functioning, under maintenance, or overloaded. HTTP status code: server is temporarily unavailable. If a service is undergoing maintenance or experiencing a temporary outage, requests may be rejected with 503.

sources.title: HTTP 503 (Service Unavailable): what it means and how to fix it | 3v-Hosting
sources.claim: The HTTP 503 (Service Unavailable) status code is a server-side error that tells you that the server is temporarily unable to process your request, often due to a temporary overload or maintenance situation.

sources.title: 503 Service Unavailable - HTTP status code explained
sources.claim: The 503 Service Unavailable status code means the server is unable to fulfill the HTTP request for a temporary reason, such as being overloaded, down for maintenance, or experiencing firewall misconfiguration. It also mentions that sending too many requests in a short period may violate API rate limits, resulting in a 503 error, though 429 Too Many Requests is more appropriate for rate limiting.

sources.title: What is HTTP Error 503 and How to Fix It - Network Solutions
sources.claim: Error 503 (Service Unavailable) means that the website's server is temporarily unable to handle incoming requests, usually due to heavy traffic, ongoing maintenance, or misconfigured settings. This error can also be returned if the agent cannot reach the server correctly, for instance, due to server settings being incorrect.

sources.title: HTTP Error 503 Service Unavailable and How to Fix it? - Scrapfly Blog
sources.claim: HTTP error 503 Service Unavailable is a response status code indicating that the server cannot handle the request at the moment. This is usually temporary and often occurs when the server is undergoing maintenance or experiencing a sudden spike in traffic. DDoS mitigation or rate limiting can also cause 503 errors.

sources.title: Agent Commands - Datadog Docs
sources.claim: `sudo datadog-agent configcheck` is used to print all configurations loaded & resolved of a running Agent. `sudo datadog-agent status` command displays the status of the Datadog Agent and enabled integrations. It also provides commands to restart the Agent on Linux using `sudo systemctl restart datadog-agent`.

sources.title: [ Datadog ] How to check the Agent's status and troubleshoot issues - CloudZ Support Portal
sources.claim: `sudo datadog-agent status` command confirms the agent's health. Commands for restarting the Agent on Linux include `sudo systemctl restart datadog-agent` and `sudo service datadog-agent restart`.

sources.title: Datadog Agent Cheatsheet
sources.claim: `sudo datadog-agent configcheck` verifies the configuration of all integrations. `sudo datadog-agent status` checks the overall status of the Datadog Agent. Commands to restart the Agent on Linux include `sudo systemctl restart datadog-agent` and `sudo service datadog-agent restart`. `launchctl start com.datadoghq.agent` and `launchctl stop com.datadoghq.agent` are used for macOS.

sources.title: Live Processes - Datadog Docs
sources.claim: For Linux, restarting the Datadog Agent can be done with `sudo systemctl restart datadog-agent`.

sources.title: Datadog Agent CLI — datadog-agent and Windows agent.exe with examples
sources.claim: `datadog-agent configcheck` prints the configurations loaded and resolved by the running Agent. `sudo datadog-agent status` shows the current status of the running Agent.

sources.title: Datadog Agent Commands with Examples - DevOps School
sources.claim: For Linux, `sudo systemctl restart datadog-agent` is used to restart the Agent. On macOS, `launchctl start com.datadoghq.agent` and `launchctl stop com.datadoghq.agent` are used.

sources.title: Datadog Intake URL Guide: Configure, Validate, and Optimize Data Ingestion - Webeyez
sources.claim: If the agent uses an invalid API key or site URL, requests may be rejected. It is important to confirm that the API key corresponds to the correct region. For the EU region, you need to use `datadoghq.eu`, and for the US, `datadoghq.com`. Datadog Agent cannot communicate with the Datadog server via firewall or proxy. Proxy authentication settings are required in corporate network environments.

sources.title: macOS - Datadog Docs
sources.claim: To restart the Agent running as a service on macOS, use `sudo launchctl kickstart -k system/com.datadoghq.agent`.

sources.title: Where are Datadog's sites located? - Daniel Compton
sources.claim: Datadog has a number of sites (regions) you can choose. For the EU region, you need to use `datadoghq.eu`, and for the US, `datadoghq.com`. It is important to get the site right as you can't change it after signup.

sources.title: Datadog API and Application Token Setup Guide | Cequence AI Gateway
sources.claim: Datadog has different API endpoints for different regions. Ensure you're using the correct endpoint: Region EU1 is `https://api.datadoghq.eu` and US1 is `https://api.datadoghq.com`. An API key is required for most API requests to Datadog endpoints, and incorrect authentication (e.g., missing API key) can lead to 403 Forbidden errors.

sources.title: 5 Steps to Handle API Rate Limits in Datadog
sources.claim: Sending a large number of requests in a short period may violate Datadog API rate limits, which Datadog signals with an HTTP 429 'Too Many Requests' status code, but can also lead to 503 errors. You should check the rate limit headers (e.g., `X-RateLimit-Limit`, `X-RateLimit-Reset`) in API responses.

sources.title: How to Set Up the Datadog Agent for Infrastructure Monitoring on RHEL - OneUptime
sources.claim: The `DD_API_KEY` and `DD_SITE` environment variables are used to configure the Datadog Agent, with examples like `datadoghq.com` and `datadoghq.eu`. If `Connection refused`, `Name or service not known`, `403 Forbidden` appear in the logs (checked with `journalctl -u datadog-agent -f`), recheck network or firewall settings.

sources.title: datadog-agent check - GitHub Gist
sources.claim: The `datadog-agent configcheck` command prints all loaded and resolved configurations of a running Agent.

sources.title: DataDog/log-lambda-forwarder-datadog/aws | multi-region Example | Terraform Registry
sources.claim: When utilizing Datadog in multiple regions, it is crucial to ensure the API key corresponds to the correct region.

sources.title: [RFC] Rate Limits API Requests - devopscorner.id
sources.claim: Datadog API endpoints are rate limited, and exceeding these limits will result in an error, often a 429 status code. The response headers like `X-RateLimit-Limit` provide information on rate limits.

sources.title: Datadog Agent Debugging Checklist - DEV Community
sources.claim: If the Datadog Agent's API key is incorrect, a 403 Forbidden or 401 Unauthorized response will be returned. `sudo systemctl status datadog-agent` checks the agent's health. `Connection refused` or `Failure to reach Datadog servers` in logs indicate network/firewall issues.

sources.title: Developer-friendly guide for sending local logs, traces, and metrics to Datadog (Part 1)
sources.claim: The `DD_API_KEY` and `DD_SITE` environment variables are used for configuring the Datadog Agent, with `datadoghq.eu` as an example for the EU site.

sources.title: Agent Log Files - Datadog Docs
sources.claim: The Datadog Agent logs are typically found at `/var/log/datadog/agent.log` on Linux.

sources.title: Datadog US1 Status
sources.claim: Checking https://status.datadoghq.com is the top priority to see if there's an outage on the Datadog side.

sources.title: How to know my datadog is setup in which region? - DevOpsSchool Forum
sources.claim: If you are using Datadog in multiple regions, it is important to confirm that the API key corresponds to the correct region. For the EU region, you need to use `datadoghq.eu`, and for the US, `datadoghq.com`.

sources.title: Datadog Agent Proxy Configuration
sources.claim: Datadog Agent cannot communicate with the Datadog server in network environments with firewalls or proxies. Proxy authentication settings are required in corporate network environments.

sources.title: Agent Site Issues - Datadog Docs
sources.claim: If your organization is on another site (region), you must update the site parameter in your Agent main configuration file or set the `DD_SITE` environment variable. For the EU region, you need to use `datadoghq.eu`, and for the US, `datadoghq.com`.

sources.title: datadoghq.eu - Domain Info - Datadog - Netify
sources.claim: The `datadoghq.eu` domain is associated with Datadog and is used for its European operations.

sources.title: Datadog Python API Docs | dltHub
sources.claim: The REST API base URL is `https://api.datadoghq.com` (regional sites may vary, e.g., `api.datadoghq.eu`). Requests require an API key and application key. Datadog enforces rate limits per endpoint and per organization, and on 429 responses, back off and retry with exponential backoff. You should check rate limit headers.

sources.title: Kumahq/kuma#11632
sources.claim: In kumahq/kuma#11632, it is reported that when communicating via a proxy, connections are not reset properly, and 503 is continuously returned.

sources.title: DataDog/datadog-agent#5418
sources.claim: In DataDog/datadog-agent#5418, many cases are cited where DNS resolution failure and Datadog API endpoint timeouts are the cause of issues.

unsupported_claims: 特に、複数環境を運用している場合に API キーを誤設定することが多くあります。(Especially when operating multiple environments, API keys are often misconfigured.)
Datadog エージェントの実行中のバージョンが古い場合、API の仕様変更により 503 が返される可能性があります。(If the running version of the Datadog Agent is old, a 503 error may be returned due to API specification changes.)
これは特にカスタムメトリクスの送信やバッチ処理で発生しやすいです。(This is especially likely to occur when sending custom metrics or batch processing.)

New Relic：UI が直感的で、可観測性機能が充実しています。503 エラーの頻度が低く、サービスの安定性が高いという報告があります。(New Relic: intuitive UI, rich observability features. Reports indicate a low frequency of 503 errors and high service stability.)
improvement_suggestions: Optional improvement: add official or primary source URLs for unsupported or weakly sourced claims.
Optional improvement: Provide direct official documentation for the claims currently listed in 'unsupported_claims'.
Optional improvement: Refine the wording of subjective or comparative claims to be more precise or remove them if direct evidence cannot be found.
Optional improvement: Clarify if the `curl -i https://api.datadoghq.com/api/v1/validate` returning 200 OK is explicitly documented by Datadog as a definitive 'normal connection' test.
Optional improvement: Review current official docs and update changed API/spec/pricing details.
Optional improvement: Add official or primary source URLs for unsupported claims.
